svg.text: When markets get wild, stop distance grows · size shrinks · 1R stays constant in $ terms

g.text: Low vol (ATR 8 pts): stop = 8 pts · NQ position = $500 ÷ (8 × $20) = 3.1 → 3 contracts
Normal vol (ATR 25 pts): stop = 25 pts · NQ position = $500 ÷ (25 × $20) = 1 contract
High vol (ATR 80 pts): stop = 80 pts · NQ position = $500 ÷ (80 × $20) = 0.3 → use MNQ
Crisis vol (ATR 200 pts): stop = 200 pts · NQ position = N/A · go to MNQ at $2/pt
Same $500 risk · different contract count · same survivability
→ Use ATR(14) on your trading timeframe
→ Initial stop = 1.5× to 2× current ATR (from entry)
→ Recalculate ATR daily · resize positions accordingly
Result: position size auto-adapts to volatility regime
